summary: Cap parsed trend BodyText by rune count rather than byte slice so the 4000-char limit doesn't split a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence introduced by html.UnescapeString.
reason: Greptile flagged a U+FFFD replacement glyph at the byte boundary on long trend bodies. Adds a shared truncateRunes helper in thparse/common.go and uses it from ParseTrendPage.
